obligations.reason: Debug interface may bypass peripheral lock controls, allowing unauthorized access to protected peripherals even when lock bits are set.
obligations.subject: Debug bus master (dm_master or DMI requests via i_SimDTM/i_dmi_jtag)
obligations.operation: Read/Write to security-critical peripherals (e.g., AES, HMAC, PKT, RNG, SHA256)
obligations.object: Peripheral registers inside wrapper modules (hmac_wrapper, pkt_wrapper, etc.)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-initiated accesses must be gated by the same reglk_ctrl lock bits as CPU-initiated accesses; i.e., write/read enables must be conditional on the corresponding lock bit.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_req_valid / dmi_req -> AXI interconnect logic (dm_slave/dm_master) -> peripheral select generation -> wrapper module interface (en/we signals)
required_evidence_slots: Proof that the debug path does not check reglk_ctrl bits before forwarding accesses
RTL trace from debug request to peripheral read/write enable signals
obligations.uncertainty: The dm_master signals appear tied off in the fact layer, but DMI-based debug paths through i_dmi_jtag may still provide access. The actual connectivity must be verified.

obligations.reason: The jtag_unlock signal may unconditionally clear all peripheral locks without requiring authentication, defeating the entire lock mechanism.
obligations.subject: jtag_unlock signal generated from JTAG hash mechanism
obligations.operation: Assert jtag_unlock = 1, which bypasses all reglk_ctrl lock controls
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock must be asserted only after successful cryptographic authentication, i.e., after verifying the correct hash via jtag_hash.
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_hash logic -> jtag_unlock signal -> reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock input -> reglk_ctrl_o generation
state_conditions_to_check: Condition under which jtag_unlock is driven high
required_evidence_slots: Demonstrate that jtag_unlock can be set without a valid hash match
Show that jtag_unlock directly overrides lock bits for all peripherals
obligations.uncertainty: The RTL for jtag_hash comparison is not in the fact layer; it must be checked whether authentication exists or jtag_unlock is simply tied high/writable.

obligations.reason: Debug mode could bypass HMAC key read protection, exposing the secret key and hash values even when the peripheral is locked.
obligations.subject: debug_mode_i input of hmac_wrapper
obligations.operation: Read of HMAC secret state (key, key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, hash)
obligations.object: HMAC internal secret registers (key, hash, intermediate key bytes)
obligations.expected_guard: debug_mode_i must not override the lock check; the lock bit should still prevent reading secret data even in debug mode.
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i -> internal read multiplexer/enable -> rdata output on AXI
state_conditions_to_check: Read enable logic (we signal for read)
required_evidence_slots: Evidence that debug_mode_i overrides or skips the lock check for key/hash read paths
RTL showing that secret data appears on rdata when debug_mode_i=1 and lock=0

obligations.reason: Fuse read via pkt_wrapper may lack lock enforcement, allowing an attacker to read protected key material (pkey_loc) without the lock being cleared.
obligations.subject: AXI bus master (CPU) accessing pkt_wrapper address space
obligations.operation: Read fuse value (pkey_loc) through AXI read
obligations.expected_guard: The fuse read must be conditional on the pkt_wrapper's lock bit (reglk_ctrl_i for PKT peripheral) being set.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI read address decode -> fuse_req_o generation -> fuse_rdata_i returned as rdata -> AXI response
state_conditions_to_check: Read transaction enable from address decoder
required_evidence_slots: Proof that the read enable path does not depend on the lock bit
RTL showing rdata driven by fuse_rdata_i when lock=0

obligations.reason: The access control matrix (acc_ctrl_c) uses an OR with peripheral 4's lock bit for peripheral 5, potentially granting unauthorized access if peripheral 4 is unlocked.
obligations.subject: CPU access request to peripheral index j=5
obligations.operation: Read/Write to the peripheral corresponding to j=5
obligations.object: The peripheral mapped to index 5 in the access control matrix
obligations.expected_guard: Access to peripheral 5 must be authorized solely by its own lock bit (acc_ctrl[5*4+i]), not by OR-ing with peripheral 4's bit.
obligations.candidate_path: acc_ctrl_c[i][j] generation -> used in peripheral select/access logic for peripheral 5
state_conditions_to_check: Identification of which peripheral corresponds to j=5
required_evidence_slots: Show that acc_ctrl_c[5][i] is used as the access authorizer for peripheral 5
Demonstrate that setting acc_ctrl[4*4+i] grants access to peripheral 5 even when acc_ctrl[5*4+i] is zero
files_or_modules_to_inspect: Wrapper modules corresponding to peripheral index 5
obligations.uncertainty: The identity of peripheral 5 and the exact usage of acc_ctrl_c must be verified in the full RTL.

obligations.reason: The reset value of reglk_ctrl may default to all ones (unlocked), leaving peripherals unprotected until software explicitly locks them.
obligations.subject: System reset condition (rst_ni)
obligations.operation: After reset, before locks are configured, peripherals may be writable/readable.
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl outputs (lock control vector) and protected registers in wrappers
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl must reset to all zeros (locked) to ensure a secure-by-default state and prevent pre-lock access.
obligations.candidate_path: Reset logic -> reglk_ctrl registers in reglk_wrapper (rst_9) -> reglk_ctrl_o
required_evidence_slots: Verification that reglk_ctrl resets to 0 (first evidence)
Check if any peripheral can be accessed when its lock is 0 but other conditions allow it
obligations.uncertainty: Actual reset value of reglk_ctrl registers must be checked in synthesizable RTL; the fact layer does not include register declarations.
